Change all four checks from `X == frame` to `frame >= X` (i.e. `frame >= bp->next_frame` / `frame >= bp->last_frame`). On fire, each handler already reschedules next_frame/last_frame to a future frame, so `>=` fires exactly once then waits — no double-fire, and iteration-safety is preserved (a moved/morphed block's fresh future timer keeps frame >= X false for the rest of the tick).
